Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Deft'S Further Answers To Interrogatories And Further Production Of Documents And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-14-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Arthur Kim, Esq., for George Bauto, Virginia Dinsay and Carmelita Bollier, Plaintiffs/Moving Party
Jon Webster, Esq., for Best Collateral, Inc., Defendant/Opposing Party
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted with modification: Plaintiffs George Bauto, Virginia Dinsay, and Carmelita Bollier's Motion To Compel Defendant's Further Answers To Interrogatories And Further Production Of Documents is granted in part, denied in part, and moot in part. The motion is denied as it seeks responses to the Second Set Of Special Interrogatories served upon Virginia Dinsay. The statement of necessity was not properly served with that set of interrogatories. Defendant's objections are sustained.
The motion is granted as it seeks responses to the Second Set Of Special Interrogatories served upon George Bauto and Carmelita Bollier. The objections are overruled. Defendants are ordered to respond further by March 16, 2007. The motion is moot in part as to the Request For Documents of the Custodian of Records as supplemental production has been made. As to the time sheets of Plaintiffs, production shall be made by March 16, 2007. As to the financial summaries and supporting documents, production or verification of reasonable search shall be made by March 16, 2007. The motion is denied as it seeks income tax records.
All requests for sanctions are denied.

Modification: Modified as to compliance date changed to 3/29/07. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
